title: The Global Brain
subtitle: Architecting the World's Universal Knowledge Graph
problem_statement: In the early digital era, the internet is a chaotic library with no librarian. Existing directories like Yahoo and AltaVista rely on manual curation and keyword stuffing, leading to irrelevant results and high cognitive load. Users are forced to browse through nested folders of links rather than finding direct answers, creating a massive gap between 'having a question' and 'finding the truth' at scale.

pillars.title: The Authority Heuristic
pillars.insight: Users equate speed and cleanliness with accuracy; a cluttered page feels like a 'sales pitch' rather than a resource.

pillars.title: The Paradox of Choice
pillars.insight: Presenting 100 manual categories increases anxiety; a single blank input field provides a sense of infinite possibility.

pillars.title: Instant Gratification Loop
pillars.insight: Wait times exceeding two seconds cause users to abandon their search intent entirely, regardless of result quality.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we organize the world's information to make it universally accessible?
How might we determine the authority of a webpage without human editors?
How might we design an interface that disappears to let the content shine?

personas.name: Dr. Aris Thorne
personas.quote: I need the needle, not the haystack.
personas.bio: Aris spends 60% of his day hunting for peer-reviewed citations. He is frustrated by the 'commercial noise' of current web portals that prioritize ads over academic relevance.

points.action: Change human-curated lists for a math-based ranking system
points.benefit: Results are unbiased and updated instantly without waiting for an editor

points.action: Put the search bar and the brand logo on a totally white background
points.benefit: People focus only on their question and don't get distracted by news or ads

points.action: Use the way websites link to each other as a vote of confidence
points.benefit: The most important websites naturally rise to the top of the list

points.action: Show a small snippet of text from the website before the user clicks
points.benefit: Users can see if the page is useful without wasting time clicking it

pivot.before: Web portals are 'destinations' filled with news, weather, and horoscopes to keep users on-site as long as possible.
pivot.pivot_logic: Value is created by getting the user *off* the site and to their destination as fast as possible.
pivot.after: A high-utility 'utility' that acts as a gateway, prioritizing speed, accuracy, and minimalism over on-site dwell time.

stages.actions: User realizes they need information quickly
Type keywords into a single box
stages.friction: Difficulty articulating complex thoughts into keywords
Distinguishing between helpful links and spam

annotations.title: White Space Maximization
annotations.rationale: Removes visual noise to reduce cognitive load and emphasize the primary task.

annotations.title: Predictive Auto-suggest
annotations.rationale: Reduces physical effort (keystrokes) and helps users find better search terms.

annotations.title: PageRank Indicator
annotations.rationale: Implicitly communicates trust by sorting results by mathematical authority.
